steps.title: Find the relationship
steps.content: The first two sentences make Paris sound ideal for Sen: she was studying France's modernist masters firsthand and beginning to build a reputation as a painter. The sentence with the blank then moves in the opposite direction by saying she longed to return to India, where she believed her art could truly flourish. The transition must signal that unexpected contrast.

steps.title: Test the transitions
steps.content: Choice B signals a result, but Sen's desire to leave Paris is not presented as a consequence of her success there. Choice C signals emphasis or confirmation, which would incorrectly suggest that wanting to return to India supports the idea that Paris was ideal. Choice D adds another point in the same direction, but the passage changes direction. Choice A means even so or nevertheless, so it marks the contrast between Paris seeming ideal and Sen still wanting India. The answer is A.
